ontologistAudit.currentSessionInsight: photonicRealism = MeshPhysicalMaterial (PBR) + PMREMGenerator HDRI (IBL) + selective MeshSSSNodeMaterial (SSS). Teaching framework migration is METAPHOR-LAYER-ONLY — VT/CRA/beats/ts-fsrs/10 InteractionMode contracts preserved. SpacekidEntity uses GLTF+AnimationMixer; SpaceshipEntity uses procedural geometry. Component Factory+Registry (OCP) preferred over full ECS for <50 entities. Drag uses proxy mesh pattern. n8ao halfRes+DPR adaptive for Chromebook 60fps.

ddd.analysis: Maps CRA/VT domain concepts to rendering decisions — domain vocabulary (Concrete, Separation) not tech vocabulary (troika vs CSS).
Domain terms: nudge, visual hint, worked example, direct instruction — pedagogical escalation language preserved.
Mayer's published principle names (SpatialContiguity, Segmenting, Coherence, Signalling, TemporalContiguity) preserved exactly.
Contrast, Separation, Generalization, Fusion, Integration — exact Variation Theory vocabulary.
Name matches user-specified domain vocabulary: '우주복 입은 어린이' (child in spacesuit) → SpacekidEntity. Domain vocabulary update from pulseShard.
Matches user-specified domain: '우주선' (spaceship) → SpaceshipEntity. Domain vocabulary update from resonanceLoop.
Preset names ('spaceship-hull', 'visor-glass', 'spacesuit-fabric') match visual design vocabulary — not raw Three.js parameter names.
Clip names ('board-spaceship', 'gesture-point', 'wave') match character interaction narrative vocabulary.
Preserves VT/CRA domain contracts — migration scope explicitly limits changes to metaphor layer only. Domain vocabulary integrity preserved.

dry.analysis: Single dispatch eliminates per-beat approach selection scattered in game-loop.
Single checker referenced by resolver, content design rules, and eval criteria — not duplicated per principle.
Consolidates MiniHudTelemetry + new content delivery telemetry into single record — no parallel truth sources.
Single compatibility matrix — resolver reads it; approach implementations don't duplicate compatibility checks.
Named presets prevent per-mesh material parameter duplication across scene — factory reads by presetId.
Single registry entry per component type prevents per-scene instantiation logic duplication.
Single DragControls on invisible proxy; all characters share same drag logic — not per-character DragControls.
Extends ContentEngagement — adds spatial fields without duplicating base engagement schema.

ocp.analysis: Open for new approach variants (WebGPU text); closed for modification — existing speech bubble path unchanged.
Tier 0 (proactive encouragement) addable without modifying existing 4-tier logic.
New signal types (haptic, sound) added via animationTypes enum; existing glow/particle implementations unchanged.
New gaps added without changing existing gap structure.
New entity types added as PhotonicComponentDefinition registry entries — Interface unchanged. Closed for factory modification, open for entity registration.
New pipeline stages (bloom, depth-of-field) added as Interface extensions — existing SSAO, IBL, compose stages unchanged.
New gesture types (triple-tap, force-press) added without modifying existing pinch/rotate handlers.
New FX types ('warp-jump', 'shield-burst') added to catalog — dispatch logic reads catalog key, no code modification.

pecs.analysis: ts-fsrs (producer) extends with R/S values; ScaffoldEscalationLogic (consumer super) reads R — new FSRS-7 fields don't break consumer.
Planet content (producer) extends TeachingBeat with new fields (audioNarrationUrl, spatialAnnotations); ContentDeliveryResolver (consumer super) reads via interface — additive, not breaking.
game-loop (producer) extends SpeechBubbleContent with referencedObjectId; speech-bubble.ts/diegetic-panel.ts (consumer super) ignore unknown fields.
Scene authoring (producer) defines component registry entries; Factory (consumer super) creates from definition — producer can add fields without breaking factory interface.
Scene presets (producer) provide material parameters; Pipeline (consumer super) instantiates Three.js materials — new preset fields (e.g., anisotropy) ignored by older pipeline consumers.
Resolver (producer) emits typed InteractionEvents; action handlers (consumer super) execute event-specific sequences — new event types don't break existing action handlers.
game-loop (producer) emits raw events; Metrics aggregator (consumer super) reduces — new event type fields in InteractionTelemetry don't break aggregation.
